Submit the draft Quality Audit Report to the Director of Quality (Lisa McDonagh) for QA review of evidence sufficiency, fairness, accuracy and consistency with other audits.
Incorporate feedback and issue the final Quality Audit Report to the Audit Owner and Director of Quality; copy the Clinical Lead and Line Manager where relevant.
For each non-conformance, raise a QIP entry triggering SOP-037 (Open a QIP); High-rated QIPs are flagged automatically to the Director of Operations; Medium-rated to the Clinical Lead.
Add the Quality Audit Report to the QMS shared drive with appropriate access controls; update the LLH Audit Calendar to show audit status as 'Reported, awaiting QIP close-out'.
Communicate findings throughout Liberty Living to support learning - via EMT meeting, CGC meeting, Board Quality & Risk Committee and staff meetings as appropriate to severity.
steps.role: Director of Quality / Director of Operations

SOP-037.title: Open a Quality Improvement Plan (QIP) from an Audit Finding
SOP-037.purpose: To open and properly scope a Quality Improvement Plan (QIP) arising from an internal quality audit finding (or external inspection, complaint, Service User feedback or incident trend), so that corrective actions are clearly owned, prioritised and managed to closure.

steps.action: Receive the trigger source (audit finding, inspection finding, complaint recommendation, feedback theme or trend report) and capture in the QIP intake log with date, source and a one-line problem statement.
Allocate a unique QIP number from the QIP Log and create a QIP record covering: title, source, description, target outcome, applicable policies, evidence references, date opened.
Identify the QIP Owner - by default the Audit Owner where the QIP arose from an audit; otherwise the most appropriate role-holder. Confirm with the named owner and record acceptance.
QIP Owner risk-scores the QIP using the HSE Risk Scoring Matrix (Likelihood x Impact); assign overall severity High / Medium / Low.
Auto-notify based on severity: High to the Director of Operations (Lisa McDonagh); Medium to the Clinical Lead (Lisa McDonagh); Low logged for monitoring.
Define the corrective actions with the QIP Owner: each action must be SMART, with a named action owner, target completion date and required evidence of completion.
steps.tf: Within 10 working days of QIP opening

steps.action: Where the QIP requires policy or procedure change, link it to the Development and Management of Policies process and identify the policy owner who will manage the revision.
Where the QIP is High-rated, consider adding the underlying risk to the local Risk Register via SOP-043; cross-reference the risk ID in the QIP record.
steps.role: Director of Quality / Director of Operations

steps.action: Publish the open QIP on the QIP Log and on the EMT pack for the next monthly meeting; the QIP is now formally open and handed to SOP-038 for tracking.

SOP-038.title: Track and Close a Quality Improvement Plan (QIP)
SOP-038.purpose: To monitor open Quality Improvement Plans (QIPs) from action assignment through to evidenced close-out, ensuring corrective actions are completed within agreed timelines, are effective and are formally signed off by the Director of Quality.

steps.action: Maintain the QIP Log as the single source of truth: each entry shows status (Open/In Progress/Closed), severity, due dates, evidence and ownership; update on receipt of any progress note.
Issue a fortnightly progress prompt to QIP Owners with overdue or imminent actions, requesting status update and evidence.
Director of Operations reviews all open QIPs quarterly per QA-22 s.5.3; agrees re-prioritisation or additional resource where slippage exists.
QIP Owner submits evidence of action completion (e.g. revised policy, training record, audit re-run, dashboard data) to the Director of Quality with a request to close.
Director of Quality reviews evidence sufficiency and effectiveness; where High-risk, consider a re-audit within an agreed timeline to verify effectiveness before close.
steps.tf: Within 10 working days of close-out request

steps.action: Close the QIP in the QIP Log with date closed, evidence reference and effectiveness check note; communicate closure to QIP Owner and EMT.
Undertake quarterly trending and analysis of all QIPs (open and closed) - by source, theme, severity, time-to-close, owner - and report to the Quality & Safety Committee.
Report status of all open QIPs in the monthly EMT pack and quarterly CGC pack (SOP-040 / SOP-041) to provide Board assurance.
Where a closed QIP is found to be ineffective in subsequent monitoring, reopen the QIP with a new action plan and re-rate severity.

SOP-039.title: Update the KPI Dashboard Monthly
SOP-039.purpose: To produce a complete, accurate and timely monthly update of the Liberty Living KPI Dashboard so that the Executive Management Team, Clinical Governance Committee and Board Quality & Risk Committee have current data on workforce, safeguarding, complaints, incidents, audits, training, supervision and service delivery KPIs.

steps.action: On first working day of the new month, request raw-count source data from each data owner per the KPI Data Source Map (HR, Complaints, Incidents, Audits, Training, Supervision, CRM, Safeguarding).

Workforce KPIs: pull Garda vetting compliance, mandatory training compliance (target 95%), safeguarding training (target 100%), supervision rate (target 100% quarterly), appraisal completion (target 100% annual), medication competency (target 100% annual).
Service delivery KPIs: pull no-show rate, timely acceptance of work, delivery to plan, quantum of service delivered, care plan documentation compliance (target 95%), 10% monthly audit sampling.
Complaints and feedback KPIs: pull complaints volume by category, acknowledgement within 2 working days, resolution within timeline, Service User satisfaction scores from feedback surveys.
Incident, safeguarding and risk KPIs: pull incidents logged within 24 hours, RCA completion, open disclosure within 5 working days, safeguarding concerns notified, High-risk register count.
Audit and QIP KPIs: pull audits completed vs scheduled, QIPs opened, closed, overdue, by severity.
Validate each raw count against source documents (sample 3 entries per KPI minimum); reconcile any anomalies with the data owner; record validation note.
Enter validated raw counts into the KPI Dashboard; verify calculated rates against targets and baselines; auto-RAG flags applied (Red/Amber/Green) per KPI Escalation & Action Plan.
Identify any KPI breaching threshold (Red); link to existing QIP or open a new QIP under SOP-037; record planned action and owner on the dashboard commentary.
Publish the updated dashboard to the QMS shared drive with version control; circulate the EMT pack one-pager summary (SOP-040 trigger).
Submit quarterly KPI self-declaration to the HSE Manager / Head of Older Persons' Services / Designated Officer per GOV-36 s.7.12.
steps.tf: Within 10 working days of quarter end

SOP-040.title: Produce the EMT Monthly Pack
SOP-040.purpose: To produce a standardised Executive Management Team (EMT) monthly governance pack covering risk, incidents, audits, QIPs, KPIs, workforce, complaints, safeguarding, finance and operations - so the EMT can fulfil its oversight role under the Three Lines of Defence model and provide assurance upward to the Clinical Governance Committee and Board.

steps.action: Confirm the EMT meeting date, members attending (CEO Shauna Delaney, Director of Operations Lisa McDonagh, Director of Quality Claire, Director of HR Claire Leavy, Compliance Manager Leah O'Brien, Recruitment Manager Karen McLoughlin) and circulate the agenda.
Risk Register section: list all High risks, any new entries this month, status changes (Open/Monitor/Closed) and any high-velocity risks flagged.
Incident and safeguarding section: incidents logged in month by severity, RCAs in progress / closed, open disclosure status, safeguarding cases (anonymised), reporting to external bodies.
Audit and QIP section: audits completed and outstanding against the LLH Audit Calendar; QIPs opened/closed/overdue by severity.
Workforce section: vetting, training, supervision, appraisal, turnover, vacancies, agency usage, working time compliance.
Complaints and feedback section: complaints volume, themes, time-to-acknowledge (target 2 wd), time-to-resolve, Service User satisfaction scores; learning identified.
Operations section: Service User count, new starts, discharges, hours delivered, HSE submissions, missed-call statements, business continuity events.
Compile the pack with cover sheet (period, attendees, key decisions sought) and circulate to EMT members; load to QMS shared drive.
Run the EMT meeting against the standing agenda; capture decisions, actions, owners and due dates in the minutes; explicitly record items to escalate to the CGC.
Within 5 working days of the meeting, publish minutes and update QIP Log / Risk Register / Audit Calendar to reflect EMT decisions.
steps.role: Director of Operations / Director of Quality

SOP-041.title: Produce the CGC Quarterly Pack
SOP-041.purpose: To produce a standardised Clinical Governance Committee (CGC) quarterly pack consolidating audit performance, KPI scrutiny, risk trend analysis, safeguarding compliance, consent trends, incident learning and quality improvement so that the CGC can fulfil its Second Line of Defence oversight role and provide assurance to the Board Quality & Risk Committee.

steps.action: Confirm CGC date, members, and agenda; the CGC meets quarterly to review quality, safeguarding and risk indicators (GOV-35 s.5.6).
Quality and audit performance section: audits completed vs LLH Audit Calendar, areas of good practice, themes from findings, QIP status (open/closed/overdue) and effectiveness sampling.
Risk trend analysis section: changes to the Risk Register, top 10 risks at quarter end, residual risk movements, high-velocity events, risk appetite breaches.
steps.role: Director of Quality / Director of Operations

steps.action: Safeguarding compliance section: cases logged in quarter (anonymised), categories, external notifications, weekly high-risk case review status, safeguarding training compliance (target 100%); quarterly safeguarding thematic review presented to Board.
Incident learning section: serious incidents, RCAs completed, themes, learning embedded in policy and training, open disclosure outcomes.
Consent and Service User partnership section: consent documentation compliance, capacity dispute reviews, refusal patterns, quarterly Service User feedback themes, complaints themes.
Workforce assurance section: vetting, training, supervision (target 100% quarterly), appraisal, medication competency (target 100% annual).
Compile the pack with assurance summary, exceptions log and items requiring Board attention; circulate at least 5 working days before the CGC meeting.
Run the CGC meeting per standard agenda; record decisions, escalations to Board Quality & Risk Committee, and items returning to EMT for action.
Within 10 working days after the meeting, publish minutes and route Board-bound items to the Board Quality & Risk Committee secretariat.

SOP-042.title: Produce the Annual Quality & Safety Review
SOP-042.purpose: To produce a comprehensive Annual Quality & Safety Review for Liberty Living Homecare covering the preceding year's performance across audit, KPI, risk, safeguarding, complaints, incidents, training, Service User feedback and improvement activity - presented to the Board Quality & Risk Committee with formal assurance to CEO Shauna Delaney and the Board.

steps.action: Establish the review scope and structure aligned to the Clinical Governance Framework (GOV-35) and the eight National Standards themes; set production timetable.
Compile the audit outturn: audits planned vs completed, themes from findings, areas of good practice, repeat findings; assess effectiveness of the audit programme.
Risk Register annual review: top 10 risks at year end, residual risk movements, new risks during year, risks closed, risk appetite breaches, board risk appetite recommendation.
steps.role: Director of Quality / Director of Operations

steps.action: Safeguarding annual report: incidents by category, substantiation rate, external notifications, training compliance, learning embedded; quarterly thematic reviews summary.

Incident and open disclosure annual analysis: incident rate, severity distribution, RCAs, learning themes; open disclosure rate within 5 working days; notifiable incidents under Patient Safety Act 2023.
Complaints annual report: volume, categories, themes, time-to-resolve, learning; Compliance Manager (Leah O'Brien) annual complaints log review.
Service User experience annual summary: satisfaction survey results, themes from quarterly feedback, partnership initiatives, complaints-derived improvements.
Workforce annual assurance: training, vetting, supervision, appraisal, turnover, agency use, working time compliance; workforce plan look-ahead.
Improvement and learning section: QIPs closed in year, policy updates issued, training updates rolled out; key lessons embedded.
Director of Quality drafts the full Annual Quality & Safety Review and submits to Director of Operations and CEO for review; CEO signs off.
steps.role: Director of Quality / CEO Shauna Delaney

steps.action: Present the Annual Review to the Q1 CGC and to the Board Quality & Risk Committee; capture Board feedback, risk appetite confirmation and any commissioned independent reviews.
Submit the annual quality assurance programme outcomes to the HSE per GOV-36 s.7.7 and publish a Service User-friendly summary on the Liberty Living website / via letter as required.
steps.role: Director of Operations / Director of Quality
steps.tf: Within 30 working days of Board sign-off

SOP-043.title: Add a New Risk to the Risk Register
SOP-043.purpose: To set out the standardised process for identifying, proposing, analysing and entering a new risk on the appropriate Liberty Living Risk Register (Corporate or Service & Care Delivery) using the HSE 5x5 risk scoring methodology.

steps.action: Any staff member identifying a potential risk shall immediately report it to their Line Manager; where there is imminent safety concern, the Line Manager implements immediate control measures to ensure safety of Service Users, staff and others.
Line Manager supports the staff member to complete a Risk Proposal Form with the risk event, cause(s) and impact / consequence statement.
Submit the Risk Proposal Form to the Director of Quality (Lisa McDonagh) for triage.
Director of Quality reviews the proposed risk and decides whether it requires risk management. Where it does not (e.g. risk minimal, already covered), document the decision in the Risk Proposal Form.
Where the risk requires management, identify whether it is Corporate (strategic) or Service and Care Delivery (operational); review the relevant Risk Register for existing risks and controls.
Gather background information and identify stakeholders, including Service User views where relevant; engage subject matter experts (sourced via CEO) as required.
Bring the proposed risk to the next scheduled Executive Management Team meeting (or extraordinary EMT if urgent) for formal review and entry decision.

EMT assigns: unique Risk ID, Risk Owner, Risk Co-ordinator (if different), Risk Criteria, Primary Risk Category (e.g. Harm to a person, Service user experience, Business/Service disruption, Compliance, Financial, Strategic), structured Risk Description, and entry date.

Calculate the Inherent Risk Rating (Likelihood x Impact using HSE 5x5 matrix - score before existing controls); identify existing controls (preventative, directive, detective, corrective) and calculate Residual Risk Rating after controls.
Evaluate risk: High (15-25, Red, not acceptable), Medium (6-14, Amber), Low (1-5, Green); identify required additional actions, assign Action Owner(s) with timeframes; calculate Target Risk.
Approve the entry: CEO approves Corporate Risk Register entries; Director of Operations approves Service & Care Delivery Risk Register entries.
steps.tf: Within 5 working days of EMT decision

steps.action: Communicate the new risk to relevant staff (including temporary), and to Service Users / representatives where appropriate (per Client Services Manager judgement); brief at next staff meeting.

SOP-044.title: Review and Escalate a Risk
SOP-044.purpose: To ensure all entries in the Liberty Living Risk Registers are reviewed on an ongoing basis, that status changes (Open / Monitor / Closed) are properly evidenced, and that residual risks exceeding agreed tolerance are escalated promptly through EMT, CGC and Board levels.

steps.action: Risk Owners submit a written status update for each open risk to the Director of Quality monthly, covering progress on actions, evidence of control effectiveness and any new information affecting Likelihood or Impact.
Director of Quality consolidates updates and reviews status of all open risks; updates Status column (Open / Monitor / Closed) where supported by evidence.
Recalculate the Residual Risk Rating where new controls are evidenced; record the date of review and the rationale; preserve the audit trail of changes.
EMT reviews all High (Red) and Medium (Amber) risks at the monthly EMT meeting; reviews Low (Green) risks quarterly.

Where an Action Owner has failed to implement required controls within the agreed timescale, this is immediately brought to the attention of the CEO / Director of Operations who then directs the necessary action or requires a progress report.
Identify the Top 10 risks across both Registers at any given time; refresh monthly and include in the EMT pack and quarterly in the CGC pack.
Where Residual Score is 15-25 (High/Red), require immediate escalation to CEO and CGC; trigger a QIP under SOP-037; consider re-audit within an agreed timeline.
steps.role: Director of Quality / Director of Operations

steps.action: Where Residual Score is 6-14 (Medium/Amber), EMT review and mitigation plan implemented; document additional actions and a target re-score.
High-velocity risks (e.g. safeguarding allegation, IT outage, fraud) trigger immediate executive notification irrespective of score; CEO and Board Chair notified.
steps.role: Director of Quality / CEO Shauna Delaney

steps.action: Comprehensive review of Registers and Risk Assessments every 6 months minimum by Director of Quality with EMT, evaluating aims, controls effectiveness, action completion, regulatory compliance and resource adequacy.
Risk Register approvals refreshed: CEO approves Corporate Risk Register changes; Director of Operations approves Service & Care Delivery Risk Register changes.
CGC reviews quality, safeguarding and risk indicators quarterly (SOP-041); Board Quality & Risk Committee receives quarterly assurance and annually reviews and approves Risk Appetite (GOV-35 s.5.6).
Close risks where all required actions are completed and effectiveness demonstrated; preserve closed risks for audit trail and consider them in trend analysis.

SOP-045.title: Engage with an External Agency (HSE / HIQA Inspection)
SOP-045.purpose: To set out how Liberty Living engages with external agencies during inspections, audits or service reviews - including HSE Internal Audit Healthcare Audit Function, NCCA, NOCA, HSE Home Support Manager site visits, HIQA, Mental Health Commission, HSA, HPRA, Data Protection Commissioner and TUSLA - so that the engagement is cooperative, well-evidenced, and managed through a single point of contact.

steps.action: On receipt of notification of an inspection / audit / review, the Director of Operations / CEO logs the notification, verifies the source and notifies the Director of Quality, who in turn notifies the Clinical Lead.
steps.role: Director of Operations / CEO Shauna Delaney

steps.action: Capture and circulate to the internal response team: the timeline of the inspection / audit, the requirements that will be inspected against, and which parties (staff, records, premises) are required.

Convene an internal preparation meeting with CEO, Director of Quality, Clinical Lead, Director of HR (Claire Leavy) and Compliance Manager (Leah O'Brien); agree single point of contact (Director of Operations), evidence pack, staff briefings and Service User notification approach (with consent).

Compile the evidence pack: Statement of Purpose, current policies and procedures, organisation chart, governance structure with contact details, Risk Registers, Quality Audit Schedule and recent reports, KPI dashboards, QIP Log, training matrix, vetting records, complaints log, incident register, safeguarding register,

Brief all staff who may be encountered by the inspector(s): how to identify themselves (per Staff Identification Guide), how to respond to questions honestly and within their role, and how to direct procedural queries to the Director of Operations.
steps.role: Director of HR / Director of Operations

steps.action: On inspection day, receive the inspector(s); verify identification; provide a working space and access to requested records; open with a brief overview of Liberty Living governance and current performance; cooperate fully and provide all reasonably required information (premises, facilities, staff, equipment, systems,
Where the inspection involves Service User homes, obtain Service User consent through the Client Services Manager before arranging visits; respect Service User right to decline.

Maintain a running record during the inspection: questions asked, documents reviewed, staff interviewed, sample IDs, preliminary findings; ensure no Service User or staff identifying details are released without consent / lawful basis.
Attend the closing meeting; clarify findings; document any preliminary findings and timelines for the formal report; do not contest verbally - reserve right of response in writing.
steps.role: Director of Operations / Director of Quality / Clinical Lead

steps.action: On receipt of the External Inspection / Audit Report (issued to the Director of Operations), Director of Operations issues it to the Director of Quality and Clinical Lead; review and prepare formal response.

Director of Operations holds overall responsibility to act on recommendations; the Director of Quality identifies required QIPs and manages them under SOP-037; the Director of Operations manages communications with the external body in conjunction with the Director of Quality.
steps.role: Director of Operations / Director of Quality

steps.action: Communicate findings throughout Liberty Living to support learning - via staff meetings, EMT, CGC and Board; embed lessons in policies, procedures and training.

Where the external body wishes ongoing updates, Director of Quality (with Director of Operations) manages this communication within required timeline; close all QIPs through SOP-038 and formally communicate plans to the HSE through QIP development.
steps.role: Director of Quality / Director of Operations

steps.action: Where the HSE issues a Performance Notice, respond promptly under CEO direction, providing written representations within the HSE-specified timeframe and taking all necessary corrective actions outlined in the notice.

SOP-046.title: Document a Visit in CRM
SOP-046.purpose: To ensure every Service User visit is documented contemporaneously and accurately in the Liberty Living Care Planner CRM and on the LibertyLiving_RecordSheet_2026_v1 home record, so that care delivery is auditable, supports continuity, and meets HSE Authorisation Scheme record-keeping requirements.

steps.action: Clock in via the Care Planner mobile app on arrival at the Service User's home; if the app is unavailable, telephone the office to log arrival and record the reason on the LibertyLiving_RecordSheet_2026_v1.
Deliver the scheduled care tasks. Throughout the visit, mentally note observations, changes in Service User condition, refusals, and any incidents for documentation at the end of the call.

At the end of the visit, complete the LibertyLiving_RecordSheet_2026_v1 entry in permanent black or blue ink: date (day/month/year), time in/out (24-hour clock), tasks completed, observations, food/fluids, mood, refusals, and any concerns - then sign with clear signature, printed name and HCA title.

Complete the equivalent visit record in the Care Planner CRM via the mobile app, ensuring entries are factual, objective, chronological, plain English, free of abbreviations (other than HSE-approved) and never use initials in place of a signature.

Where a medication prompt, refusal of care, fall, hospital admission, behaviour of concern or any other significant event has occurred, flag it in the CRM, add the additional structured entry required (e.g. medication log, incident report) and verbally notify the office.
Clock out via the Care Planner app; if any entry was omitted or made retrospectively, mark it clearly as a retrospective entry, state the reason, date, time and sign in line with IM-30 s.7.5.5.
Supervisor reviews CRM visit entries during the daily review of the round and follows up with the HCA on any incomplete, illegible or anomalous entries within one working day.
Supervisor collects the paper LibertyLiving_RecordSheet_2026_v1 from the Service User's home approximately every six weeks for office-based audit, returning a fresh blank sheet to the home folder.

Clinical Lead and Director of Quality complete bi-annual and quarterly audits respectively, cross-checking paper records against CRM clock-in/clock-out times and recorded call stamps to verify service delivery.

SOP-047.title: Reallocate a Visit at Short Notice
SOP-047.purpose: To deliver a fast, safe and documented response when a scheduled visit cannot be delivered by the assigned Home Support Worker, ensuring continuity of care for the Service User, compliance with Organisation of Working Time Act 1997 and the HSE Authorisation Scheme commitment to continuity of staffing.

steps.action: Receive the absence notification (HCA call-in, no-show alert, or planned absence) and log it in the CRM with time of notification and stated reason.
Identify the affected visit(s), priority level (P1/P2/P3), and any critical care tasks (medication prompts, hoist transfers, P1 morning calls) on the Home Support Care Plan.

Search the availability matrix for a replacement HCA who is competency-matched, geographically reachable, not at risk of exceeding statutory working time limits under the Organisation of Working Time Act 1997, and not a member of the Service User's immediate family.
Contact the candidate replacement HCA, confirm voluntary acceptance of additional hours, brief on the Service User's Care Plan, any infection precautions, and key safety considerations.
Notify the Service User and/or family representative of the change of HCA in a timely and appropriate manner (phone call as priority), explain the reason in general terms, and confirm visit time.
steps.tf: Before the visit time, ideally with at least 30 minutes' notice

steps.action: Update the CRM roster: reassign the visit, attach a reallocation reason code, send updated schedule to the replacement HCA via the Care Planner app, and remove the visit from the absent HCA's roster.

Where no replacement can be sourced within the visit window, escalate to the On-Call Manager who shall, in priority order: extend search to wider geographical area, deploy a Supervisor to deliver the call personally, or contact the Service User to arrange a deferred / shortened visit with family support.
Confirm visit completion via Care Planner clock-in/clock-out; verify the replacement HCA documented the visit per SOP-046.

Where the absence indicates a staffing shortage that may impact the HSE service for more than 24 hours or affects multiple Service Users, the Director of Operations notifies the HSE Key Contact in writing under GOV-25 / GOV-36.

Client Services Manager reviews all short-notice reallocations weekly to identify trends, recurring single-points-of-failure and HCA availability gaps, and feeds findings into the HR-29 Staffing Action Plan.

SOP-048.title: Handle Service User Refusal of Care
SOP-048.purpose: To ensure that where a Service User exercises their right to refuse care, the refusal is respected, documented, risk-assessed and escalated proportionately, in line with the FREDA principles (Fairness, Respect, Equality, Dignity, Autonomy), the Assisted Decision-Making (Capacity) Act 2015 and the HSE Authorisation Scheme.

steps.action: Acknowledge the refusal calmly and respectfully; do not coerce, override, or pressure the Service User. Presume capacity unless there is a documented capacity concern.

Where appropriate and the Service User is willing to engage, explain in plain language the purpose of the task and the potential implications of declining, providing supports to decision-making (advocacy, repeated information, accessible formats).
If the Service User maintains the refusal, respect their decision. Do not proceed with the task. Continue to provide any care the Service User does consent to within the scheduled call.
Notify the office (Client Services Manager or On-Call Manager out of hours) by phone without delay, before leaving the Service User's home, unless emergency intervention is required.

Document the refusal in the CRM and on the LibertyLiving_RecordSheet_2026_v1: which task(s) refused, Service User's stated reasons, supports offered, observations of Service User condition/affect, and any environmental or safeguarding indicators.

Client Services Manager undertakes an initial risk screening on receipt of notification, considering Service User health, wellbeing, safety, vulnerability, coercion risk, and any pattern of repeated refusals.

Where refusal may impact the Service User's health, wellbeing or safety, escalate to Clinical Lead for clinical review; where vulnerability, coercion or risk of harm are identified, initiate safeguarding procedures under HS-23 / HS-23.1 and notify the DSO Leah O’Brien (safeguarding@libertyhomecare.ie).
steps.tf: Same working day (safeguarding) / within 2 working days (clinical review)

steps.action: Where refusals are repeated, the Clinical Lead reviews the Home Support Care Plan with the Service User, family/representative and any Decision Supporter, and updates the plan to reflect agreed adjustments, declined elements and any contingencies.

Where the Service User's refusal pattern indicates inability to maintain a safe service, the Director of Operations notifies the HSE Key Contact under GOV-25 and considers whether continued provision is appropriate, applying the 3-month notice rule for withdrawal except in emergencies.
Director of Quality includes refusal-of-care data in quarterly trend analysis and feeds learning into staff training and supervision.

SOP-049.title: Hand Hygiene Procedure
SOP-049.purpose: To set out the practical steps required of all staff to perform hand hygiene to a standard that reduces the risk of healthcare-associated infection in the Service User's home, in line with the HSE and WHO Five Moments for Hand Hygiene framework adapted for homecare.

steps.action: Perform hand hygiene on arrival at the Service User's home (Moment 1 of the WHO Five Moments - before touching the Service User), before any contact with the Service User or their immediate care environment.

Perform hand hygiene immediately before any clean or aseptic task (Moment 2 of the WHO Five Moments) - e.g. personal care, wound dressing assistance, assisting with medication prompting, preparing food.

Perform hand hygiene immediately after body fluid exposure or risk of exposure (Moment 3 of the WHO Five Moments) - e.g. after continence care, handling waste, cleaning spills, after removing gloves contaminated with body fluids.
Perform hand hygiene after touching the Service User (Moment 4 of the WHO Five Moments) - on completion of any care task involving direct contact.

Perform hand hygiene after touching the Service User's surroundings (Moment 5 of the WHO Five Moments) - e.g. bed rails, mobility aids, household surfaces - and always before leaving the home, after using the toilet, after removing gloves or PPE, and after coughing, sneezing or blowing the nose.
steps.tf: After Moment 5 trigger; before leaving home

steps.action: When hands are visibly dirty or after toilet use or bodily fluid contact, wash with soap and warm running water: wet hands, apply liquid soap, rub hands together for a minimum of 20 seconds covering palms, backs, between fingers, fingertips and thumbs; rinse thoroughly; dry completely with a clean or paper towel.
steps.tf: Each handwash event - minimum 20 seconds rub

steps.action: When hands are visibly clean and handwashing facilities are not immediately available, apply a palmful of alcohol-based hand rub and rub all surfaces of the hands together until completely dry; do not wipe excess on clothing or towels.
Perform hand hygiene before putting on gloves and immediately after removing gloves; change gloves between tasks and between Service Users; remember gloves do not replace hand hygiene.

Supervisor observes and documents hand hygiene compliance during quarterly field supervision visits using the IPC observation tool; non-compliance is addressed through coaching and, where persistent, the HR-19 performance management process.
Clinical Lead audits hand hygiene compliance organisation-wide bi-annually as part of the IPC audit programme; findings reported to the Clinical Governance Committee with corrective action plans.

SOP-050.title: Use and Disposal of PPE
SOP-050.purpose: To set out the standardised process for selection, donning, doffing and disposal of personal protective equipment (PPE) following a Point-of-Care Risk Assessment (PCRA), so as to protect Service Users and staff from cross-infection in the home setting in line with HS-15 Infection Prevention and Control Policy and HSE/AMRIC PPE recommendations.

steps.action: Before each care task, perform a Point-of-Care Risk Assessment (PCRA) considering Service User condition, task to be performed, risk of body fluid exposure, environment and any documented infection status. Document the PCRA decision in the CRM where Transmission-Based Precautions are required.

Select the appropriate PPE based on PCRA: gloves where contact with body fluids/broken skin/mucous membranes is expected; disposable apron where clothing may be contaminated; surgical mask where respiratory droplet risk; FFP2 respirator where aerosol-generating or airborne precautions; eye protection where splash risk.
Perform hand hygiene per SOP-049 before donning any PPE.
Don PPE in the correct sequence: apron first (tied at back), then mask (secured over nose and mouth, nose-piece moulded), then eye protection if required, then gloves (pulled over apron cuffs).
steps.tf: Before entering care zone / starting task

steps.action: Deliver the care task. Do not touch face, mask or eye protection with gloved hands. Change gloves between tasks (e.g. continence care to food preparation) and between Service Users where applicable. Replace mask if visibly soiled or moist.

Doff PPE in the correct sequence, treating outer surfaces as contaminated: remove gloves first (peel inside-out), perform hand hygiene; remove apron (break neck tie, roll inwards, contain); remove eye protection by ear-pieces (avoid front); remove mask last by ear-loops/ties (do not touch front); perform hand hygiene i
steps.tf: Immediately after task / before leaving care zone

steps.action: Dispose of single-use PPE in a tied plastic bag in the Service User's household waste, unless the Service User has a notifiable infectious disease or significant body fluid contamination - in which case double-bag and follow specific waste guidance per HS-15 s.9.15 and seek advice from Supervisor/Clinical Lead.
Replenish PPE stock from the HCA kit; report any low stock, damaged items or shortages to the Supervisor.
Supervisor checks PPE stock and observed practice during quarterly field supervision; non-compliance is addressed by coaching and, if persistent, HR-19 performance management.
Clinical Lead reviews PPE-related incidents and audit findings bi-annually and updates training and stock specifications as needed; significant exposures are reported via QA-13.

SOP-051.title: Maintain the Safeguarding Register
SOP-051.purpose: To ensure that every safeguarding concern, allegation, screening decision, plan and outcome is recorded contemporaneously and accurately on the Liberty Living Safeguarding Log (Safeguarding Log.xlsx) maintained by the DSO, so that the organisation has a complete, auditable safeguarding register that supports oversight, governance reporting and HSE assurance.

steps.action: On receipt of a safeguarding concern (verbal, written, email or formal referral), the DSO opens a new row on the Safeguarding Log.xlsx within one working day, assigning a unique case ID, date of concern, source, Service User identifier and brief description.

Assess immediate risk: where there is risk of serious harm, take immediate protective action (remove staff from roster, contact Gardai 112/999, contact GP/PHN as appropriate) and record actions on the log.

Complete the Preliminary Screening Form (PSF1) per HS-23.1, recording: alleged abuse category, alleged perpetrator (if known), capacity considerations, supports in place, decision rationale, and onward referral decision.

Where the screening confirms a reasonable concern, refer to the HSE Safeguarding and Protection Team within the timeframes set under HS-23.1 and An Garda Siochana where a criminal offence may be involved; record referral references on the log.

Develop a Safeguarding Plan in consultation with the Clinical Lead and, where appropriate, the Service User, family, advocate and HSE; record the plan, review dates and assigned actions on the Safeguarding Log.xlsx.
steps.tf: Within 5 working days of confirmed concern

steps.action: Update the Safeguarding Log.xlsx at each material development (new information, plan review, HSE feedback, outcome of investigation, removal of staff from roster, conclusion of Garda matter).
steps.tf: Within 1 working day of each development

steps.action: Provide a monthly safeguarding summary report to the CEO and the Clinical Governance Committee, drawn from the Safeguarding Log.xlsx, including open cases, new cases, closed cases and any trends.
On case closure, record the closure date, outcome, learning identified, any consequent QIP action and confirm all records are retained in line with IM-30 / IM-05 (minimum 8 years).
Director of Quality audits the Safeguarding Log.xlsx quarterly for completeness, timeliness, alignment with PSF1 forms and adherence to HS-23.1; findings reported to the Quality Committee.
CEO reviews the Safeguarding Log.xlsx summary annually as part of the Clinical Governance Framework assurance cycle and signs off the annual safeguarding return.

SOP-052.title: Manage a Complaint Review Request
SOP-052.purpose: To provide a fair, independent and time-bound internal review of a formal complaint investigation where the Complainant is dissatisfied with the outcome, in line with Part 9 of the Health Act 2004, S.I. 652 of 2006 and QA-03 Complaints Policy.

steps.action: On receipt of a written review request from the Complainant, the Complaints Officer (Leah O'Brien) forwards the full file to the Complaints Review Officer within 2 working days and records the request in the Master Complaints Log.
The Complaints Review Officer acknowledges the review request in writing to the Complainant, explains the review process, expected timeframe (20 working days) and right to onward Ombudsman review.

Where additional information or clarification is required, contact relevant staff or the Complainant; do not re-investigate the substantive complaint - the review examines the appropriateness of process and recommendations.

Determine the outcome: uphold the original recommendations, vary them, or make new recommendations. Where a finding is adverse to a person, afford that person the opportunity to consider the finding and make representations before finalising.
Prepare a formal Complaints Review Report documenting findings, rationale, outcome and any new or varied recommendations. Submit to the Complaints Officer and Director of Operations.

Issue the Complaints Review Report outcome to the Complainant in writing, advising of their right to seek a further review by the Office of the Ombudsman (or Ombudsman for Children's Office where applicable).

Where the 20-working-day timeframe cannot be met, the Complaints Review Officer writes to the Complainant with reason and indicated additional time required; update the Master Complaints Log accordingly.
Complaints Officer updates the Master Complaints Log with review outcome, any varied/new recommendations, and triggers a QIP entry where systemic learning is identified.
steps.tf: Within 2 working days of report issue

steps.action: Director of Operations ensures any new or varied recommendations are implemented and tracked; CEO is briefed on outcomes of reviews as part of routine complaint assurance reporting.

SOP-053.title: Manage a Vexatious Complainant
SOP-053.purpose: To set out a fair, structured and lawful process for identifying, recording, managing and reviewing complainant behaviour that meets the criteria for unreasonable/vexatious behaviour under QA-03 s.13, so that staff are protected from abuse, all genuine concerns continue to be heard and investigated, and the Complainant's rights are preserved.

steps.action: Staff who encounter persistent unreasonable behaviour record each contact (date, time, channel, content, behaviour) and forward records to the Complaints Officer (complaints@libertyhomecare.ie / 01 416 3717) within 2 working days.
steps.tf: Within 2 working days of each contact

steps.action: Complaints Officer compiles the contact history and assesses behaviour against the QA-03 s.13.2 criteria: (a) persisting after the complaints procedure has been fully and properly implemented and exhausted; (b) persistently changing the substance of a complaint or continually raising new issues to prolong contact; (c)
steps.tf: Within 5 working days of pattern identification

steps.action: Where two or more criteria are evidenced (per QA-03 s.13.2 threshold), prepare a recommendation for vexatious designation, including proposed handling restrictions (e.g. written communication only, single point of contact, restricted contact frequency, security measures).
steps.tf: Within 7 working days of pattern identification

steps.action: Submit the recommendation to the Director of Operations and CEO for governance decision; designation must be authorised by the CEO and recorded in writing.
steps.tf: Within 10 working days of pattern identification

steps.action: Issue a written notification to the Complainant explaining: the specific behaviours identified, which criteria are met, the restrictions being put in place, the duration (typically 6 months in the first instance), the right to continue making genuine complaints in the prescribed manner, and the right to review.
steps.tf: Within 12 working days of pattern identification

steps.action: Update the Master Complaints Log and all staff-facing systems with the designation and the agreed contact restrictions; brief reception, on-call and field staff so consistent handling is applied.
steps.tf: Within 1 working day of CEO authorisation

steps.action: Where threats or physical violence have been used, discontinue personal contact and conduct subsequent business only through written communication (per QA-03 s.13.2 criterion (f)); report incidents to An Garda Siochana where appropriate; document all incidents.

Continue to give equal consideration to and investigate any genuine new issues raised that are significantly different from earlier complaints; not all subsequent contact is to be disregarded (per QA-03 s.13.2 caveat).
Director of Quality includes vexatious-complaint designations in quarterly complaints reporting to the Clinical Governance Committee, ensuring trends and any staff wellbeing impacts are reviewed.

SOP-054.title: Maintain the Master Complaints Log
SOP-054.purpose: To ensure the Liberty Living Master Complaints Log is maintained accurately and contemporaneously by the Complaints Officer, providing a single auditable record of all complaints, themes, severity, resolution times, learning and HSE/QIP reporting status, in line with QA-03 and QA-SOP-CMP-01.

steps.action: On receipt of a complaint, allocate a unique sequential Complaint ID and create a new row on the Master Complaints Log: Date Received, Quarter, Cluster/Area (e.g. CHO6, CHO8), Primary Theme, Severity Level, Safeguarding flag, Incident flag, Clinical Review flag, Assigned Manager.
Acknowledge the complaint in writing to the Complainant within 5 working days; record acknowledgement date on the log.

Confirm the severity level (Level 1 Low / Level 2 Medium / Level 3 High) and assess immediately whether the complaint is excluded from QA-03 (staff grievance, allegation of abuse, Cat 1 incident) and re-route under appropriate policy where so.

Where the complaint involves a high-risk severity (Level 3) or any safeguarding flag, notify the CEO and DSO same working day and update the log; ensure clinical review is flagged where care delivery is implicated.
steps.tf: Same working day for Level 3 / safeguarding

steps.action: Update the log at each material step: investigation start, evidence gathered, draft findings, response sent, complaint closed; populate Root Cause column on closure (e.g. Staff Shortage, Lack of Training, Process Gap, Communication).
steps.tf: Within 1 working day of each step

steps.action: On closure, the log automatically calculates Resolution Days and whether the complaint was resolved within the 30-working-day QA-03 target; populate QIP Raised column if learning warrants a quality improvement entry.

Generate the quarterly HSE complaints report by filtering the log for the quarter and submit to the Director of Operations for sign-off and onward submission to the HSE Key Contact under HSE Authorisation Scheme requirements.
steps.tf: Within 15 working days of quarter end

steps.action: Provide a monthly complaints summary to the Director of Quality (Lisa McDonagh) including new, open, closed, overdue, themes and severity distribution; feed into Clinical Governance Committee agenda.

Director of Quality audits the Master Complaints Log quarterly for completeness, accuracy, timeliness, and adherence to QA-03 timeframes; findings reported to the Quality Committee with corrective actions.
CEO reviews the annual complaints report drawn from the Master Complaints Log; publish the annual complaints summary in line with QA-03 s.16 and ensure the policy is reissued or affirmed annually.

SOP-055.title: Conduct an Interview
SOP-055.purpose: To set out a fair, transparent and competency-based structured interview process for all candidates applying for roles at Liberty Living, ensuring compliance with the Employment Equality Acts 1998-2022, the HSE Authorisation Scheme and HR-29 Staff Recruitment, Retention and Allocation Policy.

steps.action: Confirm Interview Panel membership (typically Recruitment Manager + hiring manager + a third member appropriate to the role's seniority); ensure panel reflects diversity where reasonably possible.
steps.tf: At least 5 working days before interview

steps.action: Prepare the structured Interview Plan: opening welcome, agreed competency-based questions linked to the job specification, sample probes, pre-defined competency scoring rubric (1-5 per competency per HR-29 s.10.5), closing for candidate questions; circulate to panel in advance.
steps.tf: At least 3 working days before interview

steps.action: Invite the candidate, providing date, time, location/video link, panel composition, format, expected duration, and offer of reasonable accommodation under the Employment Equality Acts 1998-2022.
steps.tf: At least 5 working days before interview

steps.action: At interview, the Panel makes the interviewee feel welcome and valued (HR-29 s.10.6), allows sufficient time to settle, verifies identity (photo ID), and conducts a thorough assessment of career history including any gaps in employment and credentials, in line with HR-29 s.10.1.

Ask each candidate the same set of structured competency questions, encourage the interviewee to talk, listen actively, take individual notes, score against the pre-agreed criteria, and avoid asking any questions that may be perceived as discriminatory (age, gender identity, marital/family status, pregnancy/maternity,
Invite the candidate to ask questions and provide appropriate responses (HR-29 s.10.7); provide a clear next-step timeline.
Immediately after each interview, the panel deliberates and consolidates scores into a single matrix; record rationale for any score variations; rank candidates against agreed minimum threshold.

Verify candidate's right to work (cross-reference SOP-057), HSE Home Support Worker qualifications (HR-29 s.11.3-11.5) and any role-specific requirements (driving licence, manual handling cert) before issuing any offer.
Retain all interview records, scoring matrices and shortlisting criteria securely for a minimum of 12 months following completion of the recruitment process, in line with HR-29 s.9.3 and GDPR.
steps.tf: Within 1 working day of process completion

steps.action: Notify successful and unsuccessful candidates in a timely manner (HR-29 s.5.4); offer constructive feedback on request to unsuccessful candidates while maintaining confidentiality of other candidates.
steps.tf: Within 10 working days of final interview

SOP-056.title: Verify References
SOP-056.purpose: To ensure that two satisfactory written references are obtained and verified for every Home Support Worker (and equivalent reference checking for other roles) before any offer of employment is issued, in compliance with HR-29 s.13 and the HSE Authorisation Scheme Service Specifications V2.4.

steps.action: Confirm in writing the candidate's consent to contact referees and capture referee details: full name, role, organisation, relationship to candidate, contact phone and email, and dates of association.
steps.tf: Within 2 working days of provisional decision

steps.action: Issue written reference requests to both referees using the Liberty Living Reference Request Template; cover (per HR-29 s.13.4): work relationship between employer and individual, employment dates, nature of duty, absenteeism record, reason for leaving, qualifications and any other information relevant to the advertise

Where written reference is unattainable from a previous employer, conduct verification by telephone or face-to-face discussion per HR-29 s.13.4; record date, time and full content of the conversation with the referee's verified identity.

Where neither current nor previous employer references are obtainable (e.g. recent graduate, long break from work), reference from an educational institution or other organisation with which the candidate has been associated may be accepted per HR-29 s.13.3, but only with HR Director sign-off.

On receipt of references, review for completeness, consistency with the candidate's stated career history, and any flags concerning conduct, absenteeism, or reason for leaving; record findings on the candidate file.

Where any concern arises (negative feedback, employment gap, inconsistency, refusal of reference), escalate to the Director of HR for assessment using a documented risk-management approach (HR-29 s.14.13 analogous).
steps.tf: Within 2 working days of identifying concern

steps.action: Director of HR confirms two satisfactory written references have been received before any offer of employment is issued (no offer to be issued without satisfactory references per HR-29 s.13.5).

Maintain reference records (including phone-reference notes, written references, and verification correspondence) in the candidate's staff personnel file in line with HR-29 s.13.6 and IM-05 retention.

Where references are unfavourable or cannot be verified, the candidate is informed that the appointment cannot proceed; the rationale is recorded and the candidate is afforded the right to respond before final decision.

Audit reference checking practice quarterly: sample candidate files for evidence of two references, consent, escalation use and timeliness; report findings to the Recruitment Manager and Director of HR.

SOP-057.title: Verify Right to Work
SOP-057.purpose: To verify the right to work in the Republic of Ireland for every prospective Liberty Living employee before commencement of employment, in line with HR-29, immigration legislation and the HSE Authorisation Scheme requirement for verified identity and qualifications (including the 100-point identity check under HR-29 s.14.5).

steps.action: Request the candidate to present originals of identity documents totalling 100 points under the An Garda Siochana 100-point check system, including at least one form of photographic evidence (passport, driving licence, public services card) per HR-29 s.14.5.
Verify identity by physical inspection of original documents; take certified copies for the file and return originals to the candidate.

Determine the candidate's immigration status and confirm right to work: Irish/EU/EEA/Swiss citizens require passport or national ID; UK citizens require passport plus PPSN (Common Travel Area); non-EEA nationals require passport plus current Irish Residence Permit (IRP) card and the appropriate Stamp.

Verify the appropriate Stamp: Stamp 4 (full access to labour market - typically family of EU/Irish citizen, refugee, long-term resident); Stamp 1 (employment permit holder - verify permit details with the Department of Enterprise, Trade and Employment); Stamp 1G (graduate scheme - time-limited); Stamp 5 (without condit

Where the candidate holds a Stamp 1 employment permit, confirm the permit is in date, the permit covers the type of work (HCA / domiciliary care) and the named employer matches Liberty Living - or, for general employment permit holders changing employer, ensure the 12-month rule and any application for change-of-employ

Where the candidate has resided in countries outside the Republic of Ireland for 6 months or more from the date of their 16th birthday, obtain Overseas Police Clearance Certificates for each such country, dated after the date the candidate left and covering the entire period of residence (HR-29 s.14.16).

Where overseas police clearance cannot be obtained despite reasonable efforts by the candidate (any cost being borne by the candidate), apply a documented risk-management approach in line with HR-29 s.14.17 and 14.13, with the CEO making the final determination on suitability.

Confirm the candidate's English language ability is sufficient for safe care delivery in line with HR-29 s.12.1; conduct a structured conversation or formal assessment where required, especially for Home Support Worker roles.

Record all immigration permission details, expiry dates and any conditions on the Liberty Living HR system, set a renewal-tracking reminder at least 60 days before expiry, and ensure the candidate is aware of their duty to notify HR of any change of status (HR-29 s.14.19).

Where any aspect of right-to-work cannot be verified or status is subject to ongoing application, no offer is issued; if employed and a status change occurs, the staff member may be suspended pending re-verification; failure to notify in a timely manner may result in disciplinary action under HR-19.

SOP-058.title: Onboard a New Staff Member (Day 1)
SOP-058.purpose: To ensure every new Liberty Living staff member completes a standardised, documented Day 1 onboarding process that introduces them to Liberty Living's values, structure, mandatory policies and Day 1 operational requirements, in line with HR-14 Staff Induction, Training and Supervision Policy and HR-29 Staff Recruitment, Retention and Allocation Policy. The induction is completed before any unsupervised care duties.

steps.action: Welcome the new staff member at the Liberty Living office at the scheduled start time; verify identity against the personnel file and confirm Garda Vetting clearance is in place before any further activity.

Issue the staff member with a Photo ID badge, uniform (as appropriate to role), Liberty Living Staff Handbook, copy of the Job Description, the Induction Schedule.docx and the Staff Policy Acknowledgement.docx form (HR-14 s.6.3).

Walk the new staff member through the Induction Schedule.docx programme covering organisational structure, reporting lines, statement of purpose, out-of-hours arrangements, payroll process and contacting the office (HR-14 s.6.4).

Provide access to and brief explanation of all policies relevant to the role (HR-14 s.6.5); the staff member reviews mandatory policies (Safeguarding HS-23, Consent CARE-06, Confidentiality IM-05, Health & Safety HS-10, Code of Conduct, Boundary Management HR-38, Open Disclosure, Whistleblowing QA-37).
The staff member signs the Staff Policy Acknowledgement.docx confirming they have read and understood the relevant policies (HR-14 s.6.5); the HR team signs and dates as witness.

Cover statutory and mandatory training awareness (Children First, Data Protection, Safeguarding, Capacity Act, Open Disclosure, Protected Disclosures); explain that full HSeLanD modules must be completed within 10 working days per HR-14 s.7.4.

Provide and configure the Liberty Living Care Planner mobile app on the staff member's device for scheduling, Service User information, clocking in/out, and recording reasons for early/late arrivals or departures; provide HSeLanD account creation support (HR-14 s.6.3).

For Home Support Workers: introduce the Client Services Manager and assigned Supervisor; book in the 8 hours of supervised shadowing (SOP-060) and 20 hours of induction training including 5 hours practical (HR-14 s.5.3 / s.7.3) before first independent visit.

Sign the Induction Attendance Record.docx at end of day to confirm Day 1 induction completion (HR-14 s.6.7); both the staff member and the Line Manager (or Client Services Manager for HCAs) sign and date.

Confirm the staff member's start of probationary period (6 months under HR-29 s.16), schedule their first supervision session within 4 weeks, and file all signed documents in the staff personnel file in line with IM-30.

SOP-059.title: Deliver Mandatory Training
SOP-059.purpose: To ensure all Liberty Living staff complete the mandatory training required by their role at the required frequency, that training records are maintained on the Liberty Living Training Matrix, and that staff with lapsed mandatory training are not rostered to provide care until verified, in line with HR-14 s.9.

steps.action: HR runs a weekly Training Matrix report identifying staff whose mandatory training is approaching expiry within 60 days, has expired, or is missing entirely.
Schedule the required training (in-person courses booked with approved trainers; HSeLanD modules assigned to staff); notify the staff member and their Line Manager / Client Services Manager.
Confirm trainers delivering practical components hold appropriate qualifications and have access to functioning equipment for effective learning (HR-14 s.7.6).
Deliver / facilitate the training; ensure attendance is recorded with a signed register; verify HSeLanD module completions via the HSeLanD reporting dashboard.
steps.role: HR team / Clinical Lead / Approved Trainer

steps.action: Issue certificates of completion to staff and upload copies to the Liberty Living Training Matrix and individual staff personnel files in line with IM-30.
Where staff fail to attend scheduled mandatory training, HR follows up within 2 working days, reschedules; persistent non-attendance is escalated to the Director of HR for HR-19 management.

Where mandatory training has expired, the Supervisor immediately removes the staff member from care rostering and informs the Client Services Manager (HR-14 s.9.1); the staff member is not rostered until training is verified complete.
steps.tf: Same working day as expiry detected

steps.action: Update each Home Support Worker's individual Training Plan (HR-14 s.9.5) to reflect completed training, planned future modules and progress toward QQI Level 5 Major Award where applicable.
steps.tf: Within 5 working days of training completion

steps.action: Director of HR reports on mandatory training KPIs (percent in-date by module, expired count, time-to-renewal) monthly to the Executive Management Team and quarterly to the Clinical Governance Committee.

Annual training audit by the Director of Quality validates the integrity of the Training Matrix, sample-checks staff files for evidence of training certificates and identifies any compliance gaps for QIP action.

SOP-060.title: Sign Off Competency
SOP-060.purpose: To sign off a new Home Support Worker as competent to deliver unsupervised care, on the basis of a minimum 8 hours documented shadowing using the Shadowing Evaluation Form.docx, satisfactory completion of the National Carer Competency Assessment (NCCA) and verification that mandatory training is in date, in line with HR-14 and the HSE Authorisation Scheme.

steps.action: Assign a named, suitably qualified Supervisor who will remain present throughout the full duration of shadowing (HR-14 s.7.7); brief the Supervisor on the HCA's induction profile and any specific development needs.

Schedule a minimum of 8 hours supervised shadowing during direct Service User contact, structured across varying Service User priority bands (P1/P2/P3) and levels of care complexity to expose the HCA to a range of daily tasks and home environments (HR-14 s.7.8 / shadowing 1.0-3.0).

During the observation period, the new HCA observes only and does not perform any tasks independently (HR-14 s.7.8); the Supervisor continuously observes and assesses the HCA's skills against the Home Support Care Plan, safeguarding standards, infection prevention protocols and documentation requirements.

Document each shadowing session on the Shadowing Evaluation Form.docx (Observation and Competency Checklist): date, Service User priority band (anonymised), tasks observed, HCA's demonstrated competencies, gaps identified, coaching provided; both Supervisor and HCA sign each session entry.

Where the HCA demonstrates readiness during shadowing, the Supervisor may gradually allow supervised participation in care tasks (under direct observation) - this remains within the 8 hours of supervised contact and does not constitute independent care.

On completion of shadowing, the Client Services Manager completes the National Carer Competency Assessment (NCCA) for the HCA against the HSE Home Support Authorisation Scheme domains (e.g. ADLs/IADLs, personal hygiene, pressure area care, nutrition, communication, confidentiality, dignity, person-centred care, hand hy
steps.tf: Within 2 working days of shadowing completion

steps.action: Where the NCCA demonstrates the HCA is competent across all required domains, the Client Services Manager signs off the NCCA and the Shadowing Evaluation Form.docx; where any domain is not met, a remedial training plan is created and competency reassessed before independent rostering.

Where Competency Assessment demonstrates the staff member is currently not skilled / competent to undertake the work of the HSE contract, the HCA shall not be assigned to HSE Service Users (HR-14 s.7.12); they remain in extended shadowing or are redeployed.
Update the Training Matrix and the HCA's individual Training Plan to record NCCA sign-off date, next annual NCCA due date, and competencies signed; record on the personnel file in line with IM-30.

Roster the HCA for independent visits; schedule the first follow-up supervision within 4 weeks under HR-14 s.8; conduct annual NCCA reassessment thereafter to satisfy HSE Authorisation Scheme requirements (HR-14 s.7.13).

SOP-061.title: Manage Probation Milestones
SOP-061.purpose: To establish a structured, auditable process for monitoring, reviewing and confirming completion of the six-month probationary period for all new Liberty Living employees so that performance, conduct and suitability are assessed fairly, in line with HR-29 s.16 and HR-14 s.7.14, and statutory fair procedure requirements.

steps.action: On commencement, open a probation record in the HR system, schedule the three probation review milestones (Month 1, Month 3 and Month 5), and notify the line manager and employee in writing of the review dates and expectations.

Conduct the Month 1 probation review covering induction completion, first 8 hours of supervised assignments (for HCAs per HR-14 s.8.1), policy acknowledgement, NCCA shadowing progress and any early concerns; complete the probation review template and have it signed by both parties.
Conduct the Month 3 probation review covering competency progress, attendance, conduct, integration into the team and Service User feedback; agree any development actions and timescales in writing.

Where performance, conduct or suitability concerns arise at any review, document them on the probation review template, hold a meeting with the employee, afford the employee an opportunity to respond, and confirm any required improvement, support or extension in writing in line with HR-29 s.16.5.
steps.tf: Within 5 working days of identifying concern

steps.action: Conduct the Month 5 final probation review, gather evidence on competency assessment, supervision records, training compliance and Service User feedback, and make a recommendation: confirm, extend or terminate.

Where probation is to be extended, issue written confirmation to the employee before the original six-month period expires, stating the extension length and the basis, ensuring the total probationary period does not exceed eleven months per HR-29 s.16.3.
Where performance is satisfactory, issue written confirmation of successful completion of probation per HR-29 s.16.4 and update the HR system and personnel file accordingly.
steps.tf: Within 5 working days of Month 5 review

steps.action: Where termination during probation is required, ensure the process is conducted in accordance with statutory minimum notice, employment equality legislation and fair procedure principles per HR-29 s.16.6 and s.16.7, with the Director of HR and CEO informed prior to issue.
Archive all probation review documents, correspondence and outcome letters on the personnel file in line with HR-29 s.19 and IM-30, and update the workforce KPI dashboard with probation outcome data.

SOP-062.title: Initiate Disciplinary Procedure
SOP-062.purpose: To set out a fair, consistent and procedurally compliant process for initiating and conducting the disciplinary procedure under HR-19, ensuring the rights of natural justice, the right to representation, the separation of investigation, outcome and appeal stages, and adherence to the four-stage sanction ladder.

steps.action: Receive and triage the complaint or report; document the allegation in writing including dates, times, witnesses and supporting evidence, and decide whether informal counselling is appropriate (minor matter) or formal procedure is invoked.

Where serious misconduct is alleged or an uninterrupted investigation is required, consider temporary suspension on contractual pay (HR-19 s.1) and confirm to the employee in writing that suspension is not a sanction or pre-judgement.
steps.role: Director of Operations / Director of HR

steps.action: Appoint three separate managers in line with HR-19 separation of process: an Investigation Manager, an Outcome Manager and an Appeal Manager, none of whom have prior involvement in the matter, and consider appointing a third party where internal resources are limited.

Invite the employee in writing to a Disciplinary Investigation Meeting, stating the allegation, the time, the venue (a suitable private area), and the right to be accompanied by a work colleague of their choice or union representative (HR-19 s.4); no legal representation is provided for under the Code of Practice.
steps.tf: At least 5 working days before meeting

steps.action: Conduct the Disciplinary Investigation Meeting, allow the employee to respond, interview witnesses on a confidential individual basis, gather documentary evidence and draft a Disciplinary Investigation Report.
steps.tf: Conducted promptly; report drafted within 10 working days of meeting

steps.action: Transfer the Investigation Report and invite documentation to the Outcome Manager; invite the employee to a Disciplinary Outcome Meeting, providing them with the Report in advance and the opportunity to respond.
steps.tf: Within 5 working days of report completion

steps.action: At the Outcome Meeting, allow the employee to respond to the findings, take time to consider, then determine the appropriate sanction from the four-stage ladder: Stage 1 Verbal Warning, Stage 2 Written Warning, Stage 3 Final Written Warning, Stage 4 Demotion/Suspension/Dismissal; the company may skip stages in line wit
steps.tf: Decision communicated in writing within 5 working days of meeting

steps.action: Place the sanction on the employee's personnel file; warnings remain live for 12 months from date of issue (HR-19 s.6 Warning Timeframes) after which expired sanctions are removed from the file.

Inform the employee in writing of the right of appeal within 5 working days of the outcome (HR-19 s.8); appeal grounds must be in writing and the appeal is conducted by the Appeal Manager with one right of appeal only.

Where the employee appeals within 5 working days, the Appeal Manager invites the employee to an Appeal Meeting (with right of representation), considers the appeal grounds, takes time to decide and confirms the appeal outcome in writing - the outcome is final internally.
steps.tf: Appeal meeting within 15 working days of appeal lodged; outcome within 10 working days of

steps.action: Archive all disciplinary records on the personnel file under GDPR principles (HR-19 s.8 Data Protection), maintain confidentiality and review whether information must be made available to the WRC, Department of Social Protection or Revenue on request.

SOP-063.title: Conduct an Exit Interview
SOP-063.purpose: To establish a confidential, consistent process for conducting exit interviews with all departing Liberty Living employees in line with HR-29 s.18 so that retention trends, organisational themes and improvement opportunities are systematically identified, analysed and reported to leadership and the Clinical Governance Committee.

steps.action: Acknowledge the employee's notice in writing within 3 working days, confirm the last working day, and issue an invitation to an exit interview, clearly stating that participation is voluntary and information will be treated confidentially.

Schedule the exit interview to take place during the employee's notice period, preferably in the final two weeks, conducted by a HR representative who is not the employee's direct line manager to safeguard candour.
steps.tf: Within last two weeks of employment

steps.action: Prepare for the interview by reviewing the employee's role, length of service, supervision history, training record, recent appraisal and any incident or grievance history that may inform questioning while maintaining objectivity.

Conduct the exit interview using the standard exit interview template, covering reasons for leaving, role experience, supervision and support, training and development, working conditions, management, culture, safeguarding concerns and suggestions for improvement.

At the close of the interview, explicitly ask whether the employee has any safeguarding, protected disclosure, bullying, harassment or other serious concerns to raise; escalate any such concerns immediately under HS-23.1, QA-37 or HR-19 as appropriate.
Anonymise interview themes for reporting, retain the identifiable record on the personnel file with restricted access, and ensure data is processed in line with IM-05 GDPR principles.

Add the leaver to the quarterly retention analysis, code themes (e.g. workload, pay, management, training, geography, career progression, work-life balance, safeguarding) and update the employee turnover dashboard per HR-29 s.18.1.

Produce a quarterly Retention Insights Report combining exit interview themes, turnover data and refer-a-friend uptake; submit to the Executive Management Team and Clinical Governance Committee for review and improvement actions.

SOP-064.title: Conduct a Data Protection Impact Assessment
SOP-064.purpose: To establish the process for completing a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA / PIA) for any new or changed processing of personal data that is likely to result in a high risk to data subjects, in line with IM-05 s.8, Article 35 GDPR and HIQA (2017), so that risks are identified and mitigated before processing commences and the Data Protection Commission is consulted where required.

steps.action: Stage 1 - Threshold Assessment: the project sponsor completes the DPIA Threshold Assessment with the DPO to determine whether the proposed processing meets GDPR Article 35 high-risk criteria (large-scale processing of special category data, systematic monitoring, new technology, etc.) per IM-05 s.8.4 Stage 1.
steps.tf: Within 5 working days of project initiation

steps.action: Where the threshold is not met, document the rationale, sign off with the DPO, and add a Data Register entry under section 9 of IM-05; where the threshold is met, proceed to full DPIA.

Stage 2 - Identify Privacy Risks: convene a workshop with internal stakeholders (Data Controller, project team, DPO, IT, records management, executive management) and external stakeholders (Service Users or representatives, third-party processors, advisors) per IM-05 s.8.3 to map data flows, lawful basis and risks to d

Stage 3 - Address Risks and Evaluate Privacy Solutions: for each identified risk, document mitigations applying the 7 GDPR principles (lawfulness, purpose limitation, data minimisation, accuracy, storage limitation, integrity & confidentiality, accountability) and select privacy-by-design solutions.

Stage 4 - Produce the DPIA Report: draft the DPIA report covering the nature, scope, context and purposes of processing, necessity and proportionality assessment, risks to data subjects, mitigations and residual risk; circulate to the Data Controller (Director of Operations) and CEO for review.

Where residual risk remains high after mitigations, consult the Data Protection Commission under Article 36 GDPR before processing commences; the DPO drafts the consultation submission and tracks DPC response.

Stage 5 - Incorporate DPIA outcomes into the Project Plan: ensure all recommended controls (technical, organisational, contractual) are reflected in project deliverables, supplier Data Processing Agreements (IM-05 s.11) and the Data Register before go-live.
Schedule post-implementation review of the DPIA at 6 and 12 months to verify mitigations remain effective; update the DPIA where the processing changes or new risks emerge.
Report DPIA completion, outcome and any DPC consultation to the Clinical Governance Committee and Board Quality & Risk Committee as part of the Information Governance assurance reporting.

SOP-065.title: Maintain the Records Retention Schedule
SOP-065.purpose: To establish a controlled, auditable process for maintaining the Liberty Living Records Retention Schedule so that every category of organisational record is retained for the minimum statutory and policy-required period, then either anonymised or securely destroyed in line with IM-30 s.6.9 and IM-05 s.11.

steps.action: Maintain the Records Retention Schedule as a single source of truth, listing record category, owning role, lawful basis, retention period, format, storage location, disposal method and statutory citation.

Apply the following retention periods from policy: Service User records minimum 8 years from date of last contact (IM-30 s.6.9); incidents, adverse events, medication errors and notifications minimum 7 years from date of making (IM-05 s.11.7.2); staff duty rosters and complaints records minimum 4 years (IM-05 s.11.7.1)

For categories not explicitly listed in IM-30 / IM-05 (e.g. staff personnel files, financial records, contracts), apply Irish statutory periods (typically 6 years for financial under Companies / Tax legislation; 7 years for personnel records aligned to claim limitation periods) and document the justification in the Sch

Conduct an annual review of the Records Retention Schedule against current IM-30, IM-05, HSE National Records Retention Policy and other applicable regulation, identifying any required changes and presenting to the Clinical Governance Committee for approval.
Quarterly, identify records that have reached the end of their retention period; the DPO, with the Director of Operations, reviews and approves records for destruction per IM-05 s.11.8.
Execute destruction via approved supplier using secure shredding (physical) or certified electronic deletion (digital); obtain a written destruction certificate from the supplier per IM-05 s.11.8.

Update the Register of Records Destroyed with Service User name, address, date of birth, file/record number, dates covered, former location, date of destruction, authoriser and supplier certificate reference per IM-05 s.11.9.

Audit Schedule adherence: the Director of Quality conducts a quarterly records audit (IM-30 s.9.2) cross-checking the Data Register, Records Retention Schedule and physical / CRM records, reporting findings to the Clinical Governance Committee.

SOP-066.title: Draft a New Policy
SOP-066.purpose: To establish a controlled, evidence-based PPPG (Policies, Procedures, Protocols and Guidelines) workflow for drafting a new policy in Liberty Living so that every new policy is fit-for-purpose, externally reviewed, governance-approved and traceable, in line with GOV-35 s.8 and IM-30 s.7.

steps.action: Identify the need: the Director of Quality, in consultation with the relevant Director, prepares a Policy Need Brief setting out the gap, the trigger, the regulatory or operational driver and the proposed scope; tabled at the Clinical Governance Committee for sign-off.

Assign a policy owner (typically Director-level, e.g. Director of Operations, Director of Quality, Director of HR) and a drafting lead; reserve the next available policy reference number from the Document Control Register.
steps.tf: Within 5 working days of CGC approval

steps.action: Draft v0.1 using the Liberty Living PPPG template, ensuring inclusion of: Title and Reference, Owner and Approver, Version Control, Policy Statement, Purpose, Scope, Definitions, Roles and Responsibilities, Procedure, Education and Training, Monitoring/Audit, References and Appendices.
Internal review: circulate v0.1 to the Director of Quality, Director of Operations, Clinical Lead, DPO (where data protection is involved) and Compliance Manager for review and comment.
steps.tf: Internal review window of 10 working days

steps.action: External review: submit v0.2 to the external reviewer panel (HCI Healthcare Compliance Intelligence as quality consultant, William Fry as legal advisor, and any specialist external clinical advisor required) for independent review of regulatory alignment and legal robustness.
steps.tf: External review window of 20 working days
