steps.action: Incorporate external feedback, produce v1.0 candidate, and submit to the Clinical Governance Committee for formal review and recommendation; CGC may require further amendments before recommending to CEO.
steps.tf: CGC review at next scheduled meeting

steps.action: CEO approval: the CEO (Shauna Delaney) signs the policy as approver; for policies affecting strategic clinical risk, safeguarding, or major regulatory obligations, the policy is escalated to the Board Quality & Risk Committee for ratification per GOV-35 s.8.
steps.tf: Within 10 working days of CGC recommendation

steps.action: Register the approved policy on the Document Control Register: version, effective date, review date, owner, approver, change log entry; classify as 'Active' and link to related SOPs.
steps.tf: Within 3 working days of CEO sign-off

steps.action: Issue the new policy to staff under SOP-068 (Staff Policy Acknowledgement workflow), schedule training where required per HR-14, and set the next review date (typically 1-3 years per policy type).
steps.role: Director of Quality / Director of HR

SOP-067.title: Review and Revise an Existing Policy
SOP-067.purpose: To establish a structured review and revision process for existing Liberty Living policies so that every policy is reviewed on its scheduled cycle (annually, biennially or triennially per policy type), changes are logged, versions controlled and approvals captured, in line with GOV-35 s.8 and IM-30 s.7.

steps.action: Generate the monthly Policy Review Forecast from the Document Control Register identifying policies due for review in the next 90 days; the Compliance Manager notifies each policy owner with sufficient lead time.

Policy owner conducts a triggered review: assess regulatory changes since last issue (HSE Authorisation Scheme, HIQA, GDPR, DPC guidance), audit findings, incident learning, KPI performance and Service User / staff feedback relevant to the policy.
steps.tf: Within 30 working days of forecast notification

steps.action: Determine review outcome: (a) No Changes Required - re-issue at next version with reaffirmation; (b) Minor Update - text-level changes that do not alter scope or roles; (c) Significant Update - structural or content changes requiring external review.

Draft the revised version using track changes, completing the version control change log with version number, date approved and a clear description of changes (e.g. 'Significant content updated and expanded' per HR-19 change log convention).
steps.tf: Within 20 working days of outcome decision

steps.action: Internal review: circulate to the Director of Quality, Director of Operations, Clinical Lead, DPO (where applicable), Compliance Manager and any Director whose role responsibilities are impacted; consolidate feedback.
steps.tf: Internal review window of 10 working days

steps.action: External review (for Significant Updates only or as required by policy class): submit to HCI quality consultant and William Fry legal advisors; for clinical policies engage external clinical advisor; minor updates may bypass external review with Director of Quality sign-off.
steps.tf: External review window of 15 working days

steps.action: Submit the candidate revision to the Clinical Governance Committee for formal review; CGC may approve, require further amendment or escalate to Board for policies impacting clinical risk, safeguarding or regulatory standing.

Obtain CEO sign-off (Shauna Delaney); for policies requiring Board ratification (GOV-35 s.8), submit to Board Quality & Risk Committee; finalise the issued version with date approved and effective date.
steps.tf: Within 10 working days of CGC recommendation

steps.action: Update the Document Control Register: increment version, update effective date and next review date, ensure change log captures all prior versions, and link the previous version for archival under SOP-069.
Re-issue the revised policy to staff under SOP-068 (Staff Policy Acknowledgement), prioritising staff in scope; archive the superseded version under SOP-069.
steps.role: Director of Quality / Director of HR

SOP-068.title: Issue a Policy to Staff for Sign-Off
SOP-068.purpose: To establish a controlled, auditable process for issuing newly approved or revised Liberty Living policies to staff, capturing each staff member's signed acknowledgement on the Staff Policy Acknowledgement form, and evidencing that staff have read, understood and agreed to comply, in line with GOV-35 s.8 (policy communication), IM-30 s.7 and HR-14 s.14.8 (Policy Acknowledgement Form).

steps.action: Define the audience: the Director of Quality, in consultation with the policy owner, identifies which staff roles the policy applies to using the training matrix (HR-14 Appendix 7) and confirms with Director of HR.
steps.role: Director of Quality / Director of HR
steps.tf: Within 3 working days of policy approval

steps.action: Prepare the issue pack: the approved policy PDF, a Plain English summary of changes (for revisions), and the Staff Policy Acknowledgement form including the standard text confirming receipt, reading, understanding, opportunity to seek clarification, and agreement to comply.
steps.tf: Within 5 working days of policy approval

steps.action: Distribute the issue pack to all in-scope staff via the HR system / staff portal email with a clear sign-off deadline (typically 14 calendar days for office staff, 30 calendar days for HCAs to reflect roster patterns).
steps.tf: Within 7 working days of policy approval

steps.action: Where the policy requires training (HR-14, medication CARE-20, safeguarding HS-23.1, etc.), schedule a training or briefing session and require attendance before acknowledgement is captured.

Capture each staff member's signed Staff Policy Acknowledgement form (electronic or wet signature) including: staff name, role, signature, date, and Manager/Supervisor confirmation that policy orientation has been completed.

Track compliance via the HR system: produce a daily compliance report for all in-scope staff showing acknowledged vs outstanding; the Director of HR escalates outstanding cases at 50% of the deadline window.

For staff on long-term absence (maternity, sick leave, parental leave) issue the policy with acknowledgement deferred to return-to-work; flag on personnel file so acknowledgement is captured on first day back.

At sign-off deadline expiry, escalate non-compliant staff to the Line Manager and Director of HR; treat persistent non-compliance as a performance/conduct matter under HR-19, with potential reference to the Staff Policy Acknowledgement text that failure to adhere may result in disciplinary action.

Close the issue cycle: report final compliance percentage (target 100%) to the Clinical Governance Committee and update the Document Control Register with issuance evidence; retain acknowledgement records on personnel files per IM-30 retention rules.
steps.tf: Within 5 working days of deadline closure

SOP-069.title: Retire / Archive an Old Policy Version
SOP-069.purpose: To establish a controlled process for retiring and archiving superseded versions of Liberty Living policies so that previous versions remain retrievable for audit, regulatory inspection and historic context, while ensuring only the current version is in operational use, in line with IM-30 s.7 records management and GOV-35 s.8 version control.

steps.action: Confirm trigger: the Compliance Manager verifies that an approved revised version is in effect (CEO signed, registered) or that a formal retirement decision is documented in CGC and CEO sign-off.
steps.tf: Same day as new version effective date

steps.action: Update the Document Control Register: change the status of the superseded version from 'Active' to 'Superseded' (or 'Retired' where no successor), record the superseded date and the link to the successor version.

Apply a 'SUPERSEDED - DO NOT USE - See Version X.Y' watermark to the superseded version PDF and move the file from the live policy library to the read-only Policy Archive folder with role-based access restricted to Director of Quality, Compliance Manager, DPO and CEO.

Sweep operational locations: instruct office locations to remove all printed copies of the superseded version, replace with the current version where applicable, and confirm completion through line management.

Update referencing documents: identify SOPs, manuals, induction packs, training slides and intranet pages that reference the superseded version and update to the new version reference and effective date.

Apply retention rules: superseded policy versions are retained for a minimum of 10 years from the date of supersession to support regulatory inspection and historical audit (aligned to the longest applicable retention period for governance records under IM-05 / IM-30); flag on Document Control Register with destruction

Annual archive audit: the Director of Quality, with the DPO, audits the Policy Archive annually to confirm integrity, access controls and that policies eligible for destruction (post 10-year retention) are processed under SOP-065.
Report archival activity to the Clinical Governance Committee as part of the quarterly governance report including count of policies superseded, retired and destroyed (post retention).

SOP-070.title: Handle a Staff Grievance
SOP-070.purpose: To establish a fair, confidential and procedurally compliant process for handling employee grievances under HR-19 s.10-16, providing both informal and formal pathways, the option of mediation, the right to representation, three-stage separation of investigation, outcome and appeal, and a single right of appeal in accordance with the principles of natural justice.

steps.action: Receive the grievance: a Contact Person or manager listens confidentially, offers guidance on options (informal vs formal), without role in subsequent investigation or outcome per HR-19 s.10; explain pathway, representation rights and confidentiality.
steps.tf: Within 2 working days of grievance raised

steps.action: Informal Procedure: support the employee to address the matter directly with the respondent where appropriate, or facilitate an informal meeting; aim is to resolve with minimum conflict, secure an apology and a commitment to change behaviour per HR-19 s.11.

Offer mediation (HR-19 s.12): where the parties consent, appoint an independent mediator agreed by both sides; mediation is voluntary, confidential and information shared cannot be used in any later investigation.
steps.tf: Mediator appointed within 10 working days of consent

steps.action: Formal Grievance Procedure: where informal and mediation pathways are not appropriate or have not resolved the matter, the complainant submits a formal written complaint with precise details, dates and incidents to their immediate supervisor, manager or HR per HR-19 s.13-14.
steps.tf: Submission documented within 5 working days of decision to formalise

steps.action: Appoint three separate managers per HR-19 separation of process: Investigation Manager, Outcome Manager, Appeal Manager - or a third party where internal independence cannot be assured; issue Terms of Reference to both parties for agreement.
steps.tf: Within 5 working days of formal complaint

steps.action: Consider interim measures: where appropriate and proportionate, implement alternative line management, work station change or paid leave pending investigation - this does not amount to a sanction (HR-19 s.13).
steps.role: Director of Operations / Director of HR
steps.tf: Within 5 working days of formal complaint

steps.action: Investigation: Investigation Manager meets the complainant, respondent and witnesses confidentially, in writing notifies the respondent of the allegation and issues a copy of the complainant statement and minutes; both parties have the right to representation by a work colleague of their choice or union representative.
steps.tf: Investigation completed within 30 working days where possible

steps.action: Issue Preliminary Investigation Report to both parties for review and final comments; finalise the Investigation Report and transfer to the Outcome Manager per HR-19 s.14.
steps.tf: Preliminary 5 working days response window; final issued within 10 working days

steps.action: Outcome Meeting: Outcome Manager invites both parties (separately), provides copy of report, allows response, then decides outcome - Upheld / Not Upheld / Inconclusive / Malicious / Non-Employee - and confirms to parties in writing per HR-19 s.15.
steps.tf: Outcome confirmed within 10 working days of meeting

steps.action: Where upheld, recommend appropriate action (training, counselling, disciplinary referral under HR-19 s.6); where malicious, refer the complainant for disciplinary action; where not upheld or inconclusive, no action against either party in good faith.
steps.tf: Action initiated within 10 working days of outcome

steps.action: Appeal (HR-19 s.16): either party may appeal the outcome in writing within 5 working days, stating grounds; the Appeal Manager invites the appellant to a meeting (with right of representation), considers grounds and confirms the appeal outcome in writing - one right of appeal only.
steps.tf: Appeal meeting within 15 working days; outcome within 10 working days of meeting

steps.action: Post-investigation actions per HR-19 s.17: conduct follow-up checks to ensure behaviour has ceased and there is no victimisation; archive all records confidentially per IM-05 GDPR principles; complainants cannot be informed of any sanctions imposed on respondents.
steps.tf: Follow-up at 1 month, 3 months and 6 months
